edges.description: Infection-associated molecular mimicry produces anti-ganglioside antibodies that can bind peripheral nerve ganglioside targets.
edges.target: Ganglioside Antibody Binding at Nodes of Ranvier

edges.description: Ganglioside-bound antibodies can activate complement on peripheral nerve membranes.
edges.source: Ganglioside Antibody Binding at Nodes of Ranvier

edges.description: Complement activation and deposition recruit immune cells to affected peripheral nerve sites.
edges.target: Macrophage Recruitment to Complement Deposition Sites

edges.description: Complement deposition is upstream of immune-cell recruitment and axonal injury in the acute axonal GBS model.
Recruited immune cells contribute to peripheral nerve axonal injury.
edges.source: Macrophage Recruitment to Complement Deposition Sites

edges.description: Motor axonal dysfunction causes progressive limb weakness.
Sensory axonal dysfunction causes sensory neuropathy and paresthesia.
The downstream axonal injury state is detected by neurophysiologic testing used in AMSAN diagnosis.

nodes.description: Axonal injury in AMSAN produces sensory-motor peripheral nerve dysfunction, including reduced conduction, progressive limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and neurophysiologic evidence of an acute motor-sensory axonal GBS pattern.

evidence.reference_title: C1q-targeted inhibition of the classical complement pathway prevents injury in a novel mouse model of acute motor axonal neuropathy.

evidence.snippet: Following a neuro-electro-physiology study to evaluate neurological symptoms, AMSAN was suggested.

nodes.description: Complement-fixing anti-ganglioside antibodies activate complement at peripheral nerve axolemmal and nodal regions. Complement activation is modeled as a distinct effector step upstream of immune-cell recruitment and axonal injury.

nodes.description: Anti-ganglioside antibodies bind ganglioside targets on peripheral nerve fibers, especially nodal and axolemmal regions implicated in axonal GBS variants.

nodes.description: IVIG is standard immunotherapy for GBS and is used in AMSAN cases, although some severe cases may fluctuate or require additional supportive therapy.

evidence.reference_title: Acute motor-sensory axonal polyneuropathy variant of Guillain-Barré syndrome with a thalamic lesion and COVID-19: a case report and discussion on mechanism

evidence.snippet: Current treatments for GBS include intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIg) and plasma exchange, which may not sufficiently benefit severely affected patients.

evidence.snippet: presented to the emergency room with complaints of weakness and paraesthesia in both her legs and arms for 4 days

nodes.description: Complement deposition at peripheral nerve membranes recruits macrophages and other immune cells to the injury site; this node captures recruitment rather than antibody binding or axonal degeneration.

nodes.description: Severe acute GBS can require ventilatory support; this is included as supportive care for respiratory insufficiency rather than disease-modifying immunotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: Guidelines for Neuroprognostication in Adults with Guillain–Barré Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Of adult patients with GBS, 10–30% require mechanical ventilation during the acute phase of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Acute motor-sensory axonal polyneuropathy variant of Guillain-Barré syndrome with a thalamic lesion and COVID-19: a case report and discussion on mechanism
evidence.snippet: Following a neuro-electro-physiology study to evaluate neurological symptoms, AMSAN was suggested. Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis showed elevated protein levels that confirmed the diagnosis of GBS.

nodes.description: Plasma exchange is an established GBS therapy and may be used when symptoms are severe or fluctuate after initial treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Acute motor-sensory axonal polyneuropathy variant of Guillain-Barré syndrome with a thalamic lesion and COVID-19: a case report and discussion on mechanism
evidence.snippet: After being hospitalized for 16 days, the patient underwent continuous plasma exchange (PE) treatment for a duration of 3 days.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and safety of eculizumab in Guillain‐Barré syndrome: A phase 3, multicenter, double‐blind, randomized, placebo‐controlled clinical trial
evidence.snippet: Current treatments for GBS include intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIg) and plasma exchange, which may not sufficiently benefit severely affected patients.

nodes.description: Campylobacter jejuni infection can trigger antibodies that cross-react with peripheral nerve gangliosides through molecular mimicry, providing an upstream immune mechanism for axonal GBS variants including AMSAN.

evidence.reference_title: Campylobacter jejuni Infection, Anti-Ganglioside Antibodies, and Neuropathy
evidence.snippet: GBS is most often of the axonal subtype and is thought to be mediated by IgG antibodies to peripheral nerve gangliosides that are cross reactive with oligosaccharides in the Cj lipopolysaccharides (LPS).

evidence.reference_title: Campylobacter jejuni Infection, Anti-Ganglioside Antibodies, and Neuropathy
evidence.snippet: The antibodies are thought to be induced by molecular mimicry, where immune reactivity to a cross reactive epitope in the infectious organism and normal tissue can cause autoimmune disease.

evidence.reference_title: Guidelines for Neuroprognostication in Adults with Guillain–Barré Syndrome
evidence.snippet: Of adult patients with GBS, 10–30% require mechanical ventilation during the acute phase of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: Acute motor-sensory axonal polyneuropathy variant of Guillain-Barré syndrome with a thalamic lesion and COVID-19: a case report and discussion on mechanism
evidence.snippet: The AMSAN variant of GBS secondary to COVID-19 infection is severe and can cause extensive damage to the peripheral nerves system.
